findings.description: Dedup key is the recipient address alone (key = msg.to), so a second genuinely-different message (different subject/body/template) to the same recipient is silently dropped as a 'duplicate', contradicting the docstring and losing legitimate mail.

_seen is never pruned after flush() (only _pending is cleared on line 65), so once a recipient is enqueued they can never be enqueued again across all future batches until clear_dedup_cache() is called manually — legitimate follow-up emails are permanently suppressed.

self._pending.clear() sits after the loop and outside any try, so if sendmail() raises mid-flush the list is NOT cleared and already-sent messages remain queued; the next flush() re-sends every previously-successful message (duplicate delivery) instead of resuming from the failure point.

stats.sent (and the returned count) double-counts on a retry after partial failure: messages sent in a prior flush() stay in _pending and increment stats.sent again on the next flush, so the counter overstates real deliveries.

_render interpolates msg.to and msg.subject directly into CRLF-delimited headers with no sanitization, allowing SMTP header injection (e.g. an attacker-controlled 'to' or 'subject' containing \r\n can inject Bcc/extra headers); deferred to critic-security for full treatment but flagged as it also produces malformed messages.

The SMTP envelope recipient passed to sendmail() is the raw msg.to string; if it is empty, malformed, or comma/newline-bearing the send will raise or misroute, and because of the unclean _pending (line 65) one bad address aborts the entire remaining batch.

server.quit() in the finally can itself raise (broken connection), which would mask the original sendmail exception and propagate a misleading error; a bare try/except around quit() would preserve the real failure cause.
clear_dedup_cache() empties _seen but leaves _pending intact, so re-enqueuing a recipient that is still pending creates a true duplicate in the queue that will be sent twice on the next flush.
A fresh smtplib.SMTP connection is opened and torn down per message inside the flush loop rather than reusing one connection for the batch; deferred to critic-performance.
